name: Bishal Adhikari
title: Full Stack Java Developer
bio: Full-stack software engineer with 6+ years building enterprise applications, mostly in financial services. Currently at JPMorgan Chase on the Account Management platform — Spring Boot microservices for account creation, message transformation, and rule validation, with front-ends in React and Angular. I enjoy modernization work (Spring Boot 2.x → 3.5, Angular → React) and have been exploring Spring AI and MCP-based developer tooling lately.

experience.role: Software Engineer / Full Stack Java Developer
experience.summary: Build Spring Boot microservices for the Account Management System — account creation workflows, rule validation, message transformation, and straight-through processing. Lead Spring Boot 3.x modernization, contribute to Angular-to-React migration, and built a custom MCP server and Spring AI tooling to improve developer productivity.

Developed Spring Boot microservices with REST APIs secured by OAuth2, Basic Auth, and API keys. Built Kafka producers/consumers for event-driven workflows and supported AWS/Kubernetes-based deployments.
Built Spring Boot REST APIs and reusable Angular components, integrated with AWS (Lambda, S3, SQS, SNS, ECS) and Kafka. Used Splunk for release monitoring and JUnit/Mockito for test coverage.
Designed Spring MVC / Hibernate backends and Apache Camel integration routes. Worked across Oracle and MongoDB, and supported Jenkins / Docker / Kubernetes CI/CD.

projects.name: Personal Profile Site
projects.description: This site — React 18 + Spring Boot 3.3, with a three.js hero scene, admin editor, and full Docker Compose deployment.

projects.name: Custom MCP Server
projects.description: Python MCP server with integrations for Jira, Figma, Bitbucket, and Kubernetes, giving engineers fast context inside their IDE.

projects.name: Spring AI Developer Agent
projects.description: Hackathon project — an AI agent built with Spring AI that augments VS Code and IntelliJ copilot workflows for day-to-day engineering tasks.

projects.name: Spring Boot 3 Modernization
projects.description: Led migration of an enterprise account-management platform from Spring Boot 2.6 to 3.5 / Spring Framework 6, resolving dependency, compatibility, and test issues across services.
